column1: # String values shown as 'text', integers as digits.
# Arithmetic - prefix (post-§1.4 phase 4: handlers compute b op a, so
# all-stack `c b sub` and swap `c sub b` both yield b - c, while the
# all-forward / mirror forms `sub c b` etc. yield c - b).
# Arithmetic - infix (swap form: handler computes b op a → 10 sub 3 = 7)
context set foo 99 end context get foo
def Bar refine Record [x:Number y:Boolean z:String] make Bar [1 false "s"]
def Bar refine Record [x:Number y:Boolean z:String] make Bar [y:false x:2 z:"Z"]
def Foo refine Record [x:Integer y:String] def Bar refine Table Foo Bar
def Foo refine Record [x:Integer y:String] def Bar refine Table Foo make Bar [[1 a/q] [2 b/q]]
def Foo refine Record [x:Integer y:String] def Bar refine Table Foo make Bar [[x:1 y:a/q] [x:2 y:b/q]]
def Foo refine Record [x:Any] def Bar refine Record [x:Number] (refine Table Foo) unify (refine Table Bar)
def Foo refine Record [x:Number] def Bar refine Record [y:Number] (refine Table Foo) unify (refine Table Bar)
# Table does not unify with list
# Record with inline disjunct field (list evaluated as code)
# Record with inline disjunct - unification narrows
refine Record [x:Number y:[String tor None]] unify refine Record [x:Number y:String]
# Make record with inline disjunct - none value
def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:[String tor None]] make Foo [42 None]
# Make record with inline disjunct - string value
def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:[String tor None]] make Foo [42 "hello"]
# Record with user-defined type Field (disjunct for optional)
def OptStr (String tor None) def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:OptStr] Foo
# Record with user-defined type - unification narrows disjunct
def OptStr (String tor None) def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:OptStr] Foo unify refine Record [x:Number y:String]
# Make record with optional field - string value
def OptStr (String tor None) def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:OptStr] make Foo [42 "hello"]
# Make record with optional field - none value
def OptStr (String tor None) def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:OptStr] make Foo [42 None]
# Def map with disjunct value field — `y:(String tor None)` accepts
# only `String` or `None`, not absence (Absent is not in the disjunct).
def a {x:Integer y:(String tor None)} a unify {x:1}
def a {x:Integer y:(String tor None)} a unify {x:1,y:"Y"}
def a {x:Integer y:(String tor None)} a unify {x:1,y:None}
# Make record from map - all fields provided
def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:String] make Foo {x:1 y:"hi"}
# Make record from map - optional field provided
def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:[String tor None]] make Foo {x:1 y:"hi"}
# Make record from map - optional field omitted (defaults to none)
def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:[String tor None]] make Foo {x:1}
# Make record from named list - optional field omitted (defaults to none)
def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:[String tor None]] make Foo [x:1]
# Make record from map - missing required field is an error
def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:String] make Foo {x:1}
# Make record from map - unknown field is an error
def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:String] make Foo {x:1 y:"hi" z:3}
# Options unify - concrete defaults fill in
# Options with type-only field - missing fails
# Options with type Field - provided value succeeds
def d (make Options {y:(Integer tor String)}) d unify {y:1}
def d (make Options {y:(Integer tor String)}) d unify {y:"Y"}
# Options with None in disjunct - absence defaults to None
def d (make Options {y:(Integer tor None)}) d unify {}
# Optional field syntax ? — desugars to (type Tor None Tor Absent).
# Absent is the kernel type meaning "key not present"; its appearance
# as a disjunct alternative makes "may be missing" first-class.
# Computed key syntax {[key]:value} — uses defined variables
# Computed key syntax — evaluated key expression
# Optional function arguments — named optional param
def add2 fn [[x:(Integer tor None) y:Integer] [Integer] [x add y]] add2 5
def add2 fn [[x:(Integer tor None) y:Integer] [Integer] [x add y]] add2 10 5
# Optional function arguments — named with ? list pair syntax
def add3 fn [[x?:Integer y:Integer] [Integer] [x add y]] add3 5
def add3 fn [[x?:Integer y:Integer] [Integer] [x add y]] add3 10 5
# Optional function arguments — unnamed optional param
def addopt fn [[x?:Integer y:Integer] [Integer] [x add y]] addopt 7
def addopt fn [[x?:Integer y:Integer] [Integer] [x add y]] addopt 3 7
# Multiple optional args — 2 optional, 1 required (2^2=4 sigs)
def f3 fn [[a?:Integer b?:Integer c:Integer] [Integer] [a add b add c]] f3 1
def f3 fn [[a?:Integer b?:Integer c:Integer] [Integer] [a add b add c]] f3 10 1
def f3 fn [[a?:Integer b?:Integer c:Integer] [Integer] [a add b add c]] f3 10 20 1
# Multiple optional args — all optional (same types), 1 or 2 args
def f4 fn [[a?:Integer b?:Integer] [Integer] [a add b]] f4 5
def f4 fn [[a?:Integer b?:Integer] [Integer] [a add b]] f4 5 3
def zero fn [[a?:Integer b?:Integer] [Integer] [a add b]] zero
def zero fn [[a?:Integer b?:Integer] [Integer] [a add b]] zero 5
def zero fn [[a?:Integer b?:Integer] [Integer] [a add b]] zero 5 3
# 0-arg function call — no optional params (non-optional 0-arg fn)
# 0-arg function call — mixed optional and required
# 0-arg call — single optional with different types
# Table with optional field - mixed string and none values
def OptStr (String tor None) def Foo refine Record [x:Number y:OptStr] def Bar refine Table Foo make Bar [[1 "a"] [2 None]]
# Record with nested user-defined record type
def Inner refine Record [z:String] def Outer refine Record [a:Number b:Inner] Outer
# Do - evaluate map list values (multi-word expressions need lists)
# Do - evaluate map word values (single words resolve directly)
# typeof - returns a Type literal of the value's exact type. For
# concrete values, the leaf type (Integer, String, Boolean, …); for
# type literals, the metatype (None has 1 part so it reports Type).
# is - type/value check: true if a unify b -> a
# is - typed list: instance matches typed list constraint
# is - typed map: instance matches typed map constraint
# is - concrete map matches map with type-constrained values
# is - closed map: extra keys cause failure
# is - closed list: length must match
# teq - strict type equality (lattice identity), not subtype
# teq - non-types return false on either side
# teq - structural type equality (record / disjunct / typed-list)
# tpartial - wrap every record field as T|None
# tpartial - idempotent on fields already including None
# is - per-element type List: type Names in lists match element-wise
# is - booleans in lists resolve correctly
# is - atoms in lists (bare words never degrade — use /q for atom data)
# is - record type Equality (records only unify with records)
# Use parens to scope each record expression so `is` gets both as args.
# is - table type Equality (tables only unify with tables)
def R refine Record [x:Number] (refine Table R) is (refine Table R)
# is - Node type Hierarchy (values use concrete List/Map types)
refine Record [x:Number y:String] eq refine Record [x:Number y:String]
refine Record [x:Number y:String] eq refine Record [x:Number y:Number]
refine Record [x:Number y:String] neq refine Record [x:Number y:Number]
refine Record [x:Number] eq refine Record [x:Number y:String]
def R refine Record [x:Number] (refine Table R) eq (refine Table R)
# eq/neq - concrete lists & maps compare by identity (same container):
# two separate literals are distinct, a value dup'd from one is the same.
def Foo [:Number] def Bar [:Number] Foo eq Bar
def Foo [:Number] def Bar [:String] Foo neq Bar
# base - returns zero/default value for a type
# make record with base:true - missing fields get zero values
def P refine Record [x:Number y:String] make P {x:1} {base:true}
# make record with base:true - all fields provided
def P refine Record [x:Number y:String] make P {x:1 y:"hi"} {base:true}
# make record with base:true - optional field gets base not none
def P refine Record [x:Number y:[String tor None]] make P {x:1} {base:true}
# make record with base:true - boolean field
def P refine Record [x:Number y:Boolean] make P {x:1} {base:true}
# make record with base:true - multiple missing fields
def P refine Record [x:Number y:String z:Boolean] make P {x:1} {base:true}
# make record with base:true - named list form
def P refine Record [x:Number y:String] make P [x:1] {base:true}
# type Name uniqueness - conflicting parts are rejected

column2: ERROR:type: name part "String" in "Foo/String" conflicts with an existing type name

column1: # Comparison - cross-branch scalars order Atom < Boolean < Number < String < Path
# Comparison - total order: cross-branch by precedence
# (Never < Any < None < Word < Type < Scalar < Node < Ideal); same
# branch by size, then type (List < Map), then element-wise
# Comparison - forward style (swap convention: args[0]=1, args[1]=2, compare(args[1],args[0]))
# if - 3-arg with scalar condition and scalar branches
# if - 3-arg with scalar condition, true branch only returned
# if - only matching branch evaluated (no error from unevaluated branch)
# if - truthy values: non-zero, non-empty string, true
# def creates a word whose body is the partial application of another word.
# Arithmetic currying (brackets needed for sequential planner Case D).
# Curried form `[op K] end N op` is the swap form `N op K`, which under
# §1.4 phase 4 yields b op a → N op K.
def inc word [1 add] 1 inc inc inc
# Curried boolean operators (brackets for sequential planner)
def and_true word [and true] end false and_true
def or_true word [or true] end false or_true
# Curried comparison operators (brackets for sequential planner)
# Curried string concatenation (brackets for sequential planner).
# Swap form `N op K`: handler computes args[1]+args[0] = "world"+"hello ".
# Chaining curried words (brackets for sequential planner)
def add5 word [add 5] end def mul2 word [mul 2] end 3 add5 mul2
def mul2 word [mul 2] end def add5 word [add 5] end 3 mul2 add5
# Curried words in parentheses (brackets for sequential planner)
def add5 word [add 5] end (3 add5) mul 2
def add5 word [add 5] end def add5_twice word [add5 add5] 10 add5_twice
# Dot - key extraction from maps (prefix form)
# Dot - key extraction from maps (forward form)
# Dot - nested map access (chained forward form)
# Dot - null safety (none parent returns none)
# Dot - list out of bounds returns none
# Dot - integer key on map (converted to string)
# Dot - integer key missing from map returns none
# Dot notation - with def storing map
# Dot notation - missing key returns none (def)
# Dot notation - null propagation through chain (def)
# Dot notation - store requires explicit get
context set sv {x:42} end (context get sv) . x
# Dotr - strict key extraction (errors on missing)
# Dotr - missing key is an error

column1: # Convert to string - base in settings map
# quote converts known words to atoms
# def body is implicitly quoted (not auto-evaluated)
def triple word [dup dup add add] 3 triple
# String interpolation with backtick template strings
# size - natural size via the Sizer behaviour
def Pt (refine Object {x:Integer y:Integer}); size (make Pt {x:1 y:2})
